blocks:
Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ إِذَا طَلَّقْتُمُ ٱلنِّسَآءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ وَأَحْصُوا۟ ٱلْعِدَّةَ ۖ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ رَبَّكُمْ ۖ لَا تُخْرِجُوهُنَّ مِنۢ بُيُوتِهِنَّ وَلَا يَخْرُجْنَ إِلَّآ أَن يَأْتِينَ بِفَـٰحِشَةٍ مُّبَيِّنَةٍ ۚ وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَ ٱللَّهِ فَقَدْ ظَلَمَ نَفْسَهُۥ ۚ لَا تَدْرِى لَعَلَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ أَمْرًا (۱)
O Prophet! When you divorce women, divorce them at [the conclusion of] their term and calculate the term, and be wary of Allah, your Lord. Do not turn them out from their homes, nor shall they go out, unless they commit a gross indecency. These are Allah’s bounds, and whoever transgresses the bounds of Allah certainly wrongs himself. You never know maybe Allah will bring off something new later on. (1)

Key Points

Aḥṣū (أحصوا) is derived from iḥṣāʾ (اِحصاء), meaning counting. Its root, ḥiṣā (حِصى), means pebbles, because in the past, accounts were kept using sand and pebbles. (1)

The meaning of fāḥishatin mubayyinah (فاحشة مبیّنة) is committing adultery or harming the household members, which permits the expulsion of the divorced wife from the house. (2)

From the moment a man divorces his wife, the woman must wait until three menstrual cycles and purification from them have passed, and then she may choose another husband if she wishes. This time interval is called ‘iddah (waiting period), and the woman must keep track of it herself. During the ‘iddah period, the man is obligated to pay the maintenance (nafaqah) of the divorced wife and cannot expel her from the house in a revocable divorce (ṭalāq raj‘ī). Of course, in this situation, the woman also cannot leave the house without the man's permission.

During the ‘iddah period, the man has the right to reconcile with his wife and resume life together without the need for a new marriage contract. Perhaps one of the philosophies behind establishing the ‘iddah and this three-month interval for the woman, especially while she remains in the husband's house, is precisely this possibility of return, so that separation does not occur easily and hastily. "لعلّ اللّه یحدث بعد ذلك امراً" (Perhaps Allah will bring about a new matter after that).

Islam has imposed restrictions to prevent unnecessary divorce. Among these is that reconciliation with the wife after three divorces becomes permanently forbidden, and they cannot reconcile. After that, the man can only remarry her if a muḥallil (intermediary husband) is involved. We read in a tradition that Imam Riḍā عليه السلام said regarding the reason for needing a muḥallil: "So that people do not take divorce lightly and simply: لئلا یوقع النّاس الاستخفاف بالطّلاق" (3)

The Quran states: "فامساكٌ بالمعروف" (then retain [her] according to what is acceptable), meaning keep your wife with kindness, "او تسریح باحسان" (or release [her] with kindness), meaning separate with goodness. No one has the right to retain a woman through mistreatment, in which case the Islamic ruler will protect the woman.

A Look at the Issue of Divorce

In Islam, divorce is the most detested of permissible acts. (4) The Noble Prophet of Islam says: Marry and do not divorce, for divorce causes the Throne of God to tremble: "تزوّجوا و لا تطلّقوا فانّ الطلاق یهتزّ منه العرش" (5) Of course, divorce is a solution and sometimes a necessity.

Regarding the manner of divorce, several theories exist:

A) Divorce should be easily executable and without any restriction. This leads to abuse by frivolous and pleasure-seeking individuals who desire women only for their fleeting desires.

B) Divorce should be completely forbidden. The Church advocates this theory. However, the prohibition of divorce causes men and women who are incompatible to suffer and endure until the end of their lives, or secretly take another spouse.

C) Divorce should be in the hands of the woman. In this scenario, divorce rates increase because women are more emotional and sensitive, and they resort to divorce when facing life's difficulties, causing many lives to fall apart over minor distress.

D) Divorce should be in the hands of the man, but within the framework of law and under the full supervision of the Islamic ruler, who is responsible for preventing oppression and establishing justice. Of course, in cases where the woman requests the divorce, the Islamic ruler addresses her request and, if necessary, issues a decree for divorce and separation.

In Islam, divorce must adhere to specific conditions and laws and be bound by ethical issues. Just as war with the enemy has conditions, and you do not have the right to poison drinking water, destroy trees, or oppress children, women, the elderly, or prisoners.

Factors and Grounds for Conflict and Separation

  1. Bad Temper/Ill-Manner

One of the most important factors in divorce is the bad temper of the husband and wife towards each other. We read in a narration that Imam Ali عليه السلام said: Whoever has a bad temper, his family will be distressed by him. "من ساء خلقه ملّهُ اهله" (6)

  1. Lustfulness

Islam advises men and women to refrain from ogling and looking at non-mahram (unrelated) individuals with lustful intent. Furthermore, it advises women to cover themselves from non-mahram men and not engage in display and coquetry in public.

Imam Ṣādiq عليه السلام said: How many a brief glance has resulted in long regret. "كم من نظرةٍ اورثت حسرةً طویلة" (7)

And in another tradition, He said: Be chaste regarding the women of the people, so that the chastity of your women may be preserved. "عفّوا عن نساء الناس یعفّ عن نساءكم" (8)

It is narrated in traditions that during the time of Prophet David عليه السلام, a man had an illicit relationship with a woman. God inspired that woman to tell the man: Whenever you violate a woman, a man will violate your wife. When the man returned home, he found a stranger with his wife. He arrested him and brought him to Prophet David, complaining. God revealed to Prophet David to tell him: As you give, so shall you receive. (9)

  1. Neglect by the Husband and Wife of Each Other's Needs

The Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله says: A man sitting with his family is more beloved to Allah the Almighty than performing i‘tikāf (spiritual retreat) in this mosque of mine. "جلوس المرء عند عیاله احبُّ الى اللّه تعالى من الاعتكاف فى مسجدى هذا" (10)

Imam Ṣādiq عليه السلام said: It is not appropriate for a woman to neglect herself and abandon the adornment and beauty that sweetens family life. "لاینبغى للمرئة ان تعطّل نفسها" (11)

Ḥasan ibn Jahm says: I saw Imam Kāẓim عليه السلام while He had dyed His hair. The Imam عليه السلام, seeing my surprise, said: The husband's adornment increases the chastity of women, and women abandon chastity when their husbands neglect their appearance. "ان التهیئة ممّا یزید فى عفّة النّساء و لقد ترك النّساء العفة لترك ازواجهن التهیئة" Then He said: Just as you dislike seeing your wives without adornment, they are the same. (12)

Ways to Resolve Conflict Before Divorce

  1. Family Court (Arbitration)

Islam has recommended that in cases of conflict between husband and wife, caring individuals from both sides be chosen as arbitrators (ḥakam) to investigate the conflict and resolve it.

In this method, conflicts are resolved in the initial stages, and the couple's matter will not escalate to divorce and court. "و ان خفتم شقاق بینهما فابعثوا حَكماً من اهله و حَكماً من اهلها..." (And if you fear dissension between the two, send an arbitrator from his people and an arbitrator from her people...) (13)

  1. Presence of Two Just Witnesses

Based on Islamic rulings, for a divorce to take place, two just witnesses must be present to attest to the divorce. This acts as an obstacle to divorce because:

Firstly: The detestation and ugliness of divorce in society usually cause individuals to refrain from being witnesses to a divorce and the dissolution of a family.

Secondly: Individuals invited as witnesses often strive to establish reconciliation and peace between the couple.

  1. Establishing the ‘Iddah (Waiting Period)

The ‘iddah is essentially a delay between the decision and the action. Islam instructs men to keep their divorced wives in the house until the end of the ‘iddah period and bear their expenses, including food and clothing. It is natural that the interaction and companionship of the man with his divorced wife during this period cause their emotions to subside, allowing them to reflect on the consequences of divorce with greater opportunity and care. It is quite possible that during this time, the couple will resume their shared life.

Imam Bāqir عليه السلام says: The divorced woman who is in her husband's house should apply kohl, dye her hands (with henna), use perfume, and wear the clothes the man likes, so that they may decide with greater wisdom and prudence, and he may reconcile with his wife and resume life. "المطلقة تكتحل و تختضب و تطیب و تلبس ما شانت من الثیاب، لان اللّه عزّ و جلّ یقول لعلّ اللّه یحدث بعد ذلك امراً لعلّها ان تقع فى نفسه فیراجعها" (14)

The Philosophy of the Divorce ‘Iddah

There is no doubt that all divine rulings possess wisdom and benefit, which can be attained or by which corruption can be prevented through performing or abandoning that act, even if we do not know all of them. Among these is the command for a woman to observe the ‘iddah upon divorce, for which several wisdoms can be stated with a little reflection:

  1. Preserving the sanctity of matrimony. Islam considers marriage a sacred matter and assigns a boundary and scope to it, and observing the ‘iddah helps preserve this sanctity.

  2. Lineage of children. Observing the ‘iddah prevents the mixing of sperm from different husbands, thereby preserving the child's attribution to his father, which is the source of numerous rights.

  3. A grace period for returning to life together.

Furthermore, the husband's reconciliation with the wife he divorced has advantages over remarrying, including:

A) Life with a new wife requires new experience, but life with his former wife is life accompanied by experience.

B) Seeking a new wife imposes new costs on the man.

C) Sometimes, returning to the previous life preserves the social standing of the man and woman in society and removes the resentments caused by the divorce.

D) Children whose warm family nucleus was dissolved due to divorce return to the embrace of the family, and the psychological damage they suffered due to the divorce is compensated.

E) Sometimes, returning to life together fosters the spirit of forgiveness, patience, and sacrifice in life.

(1) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma‘ani. (2) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. (3) Uyun al-Akhbar, Vol. 2, p. 85. (4) Wasā’il al-Shī‘ah, Vol. 15, p. 266. (5) Wasā’il al-Shī‘ah, Vol. 15, p. 268. (6) Mizan al-Hikmah, Hadith 5103. (7) Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 559. (8) Amali of Shaykh Ṣadūq, p. 238. (9) Man Lā Yaḥḍuruhu al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, p. 22. (10) Mizan al-Hikmah, Hadith 7884. (11) Majmu‘ah Warrām, Vol. 2, p. 121. (12) Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 567. (13) An-Nisa (4): 35. (14) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 5, p. 352.

Messages

1- Addressing the Prophet in the conveyance of general commands, which are directed at the public, is both a sign of respect for the Prophet and an indication of the necessity for His supervision over the proper implementation of divine limits. "یا ایها النبى اذا طلقتم"

2- To express the importance of the command and create motivation in others, address the primary person. "یا ایها النبى اذا طلقتم"

3- The marriage contract has no specific time, but divorce must occur at a time when the start of the ‘iddah is possible, meaning the woman is not menstruating, and the man has not had intercourse with his wife during the period of purity following menstruation. "فطلّقوهنّ لعدّتهن"

4- Divorce is in the hands of the man. "طلقتم, طلقوهنّ"

5- To preserve the family structure, the law of divorce has been equipped with conditions that act as speed bumps. "طلّقوهنّ لعدّتهن"

6- We must carefully observe the timing in divine rulings. "احصوا العدّة"

7- Separation and divorce must not be accompanied by sin. "طلقتم... اتقوا اللّه"

8- Observing piety (taqwā) in the matter of divorce, especially regarding the ‘iddah, is necessary for both the man and the woman. "احصوا العدّة و اتّقوا اللّه"

9- Divine Lordship necessitates caution and piety. "اتقوا اللّه ربّكم"

10- The divorced woman, as long as she is observing the ‘iddah, is considered to be residing in her own home, and her right to residence is not revoked. "لاتخرجوهنّ من بیوتهنّ"

11- Divorce must not be accompanied by insult or humiliation of the woman. "لاتخرجوهنّ من بیوتهنّ"

12- Block the paths of conferring favors and seeking favors. So that the husband does not confer a favor and the wife does not seek one, God says: It is her own house. "من بیوتهنّ"

13- Women should not view divorce as the end of the matter; they should remain in the house, perhaps the man will change his mind and reconcile with them. "لایخرجن"

14- Burning bridges and closing the paths to reconciliation is oppression against oneself. "لاتخرجوهنّ... لایخرجن... فقد ظلم نفسه"

15- A shameless sinner who has violated boundaries has no sanctity and deserves humiliation. "لا تخرجوهنّ... الاّ أن یأتین بفاحشة مبیّنة"

16- Misuse of the law is forbidden. (The woman cannot misuse her right to residence and turn the house into an environment for immorality and vice to take revenge on her husband). "الاّ ان یأتین بفاحشة مبیّنة"

17- Expulsion of the woman from the house is conditional upon her immorality being clear and proven; suspicion is not sufficient. "الاّ ان یأتین بفاحشة مبیّنة"

18- Men should not confer favors upon their divorced wives for providing maintenance and housing, because this is the ruling of God. "تلك حدود اللّه"

19- Women should not worry about being restricted to the house, because this restriction has been decreed by the Wise God. "تلك حدود اللّه"

20- Ignoring the commands of God and breaking the divine limits is oppression against oneself, not against God. "و من یتعدّ حدود اللّه فقد ظلم نفسه"

21- We make decisions based on our information and knowledge, but we are unaware of the future. By relying on God, we should be hopeful about the future. "لا تدرى لعلّ اللّه..."

22- Despair is forbidden in Islam, and a Muslim person must be optimistic about the future. "لعل اللّه یحدث بعد ذلك امراً" (At the time of divorce, when both parties are worried, they need reassurance and comfort.)

23- We should take bitter events as a good omen. "طلّقتم النساء... لعلّ اللّه یحدث بعد ذلك امراً"

24- Observe piety and the divine limits, and entrust the future to God. "اتقوا اللّه... تلك حدود اللّه... لعل اللّه یحدث بعد ذلك امراً"

25- Do not always confine divine favor to the current situation; perhaps separations become the factor leading to a more desirable situation. "لعل اللّه یحدث بعد ذلك امراً"

Explanations

Portrait of Surah At-Talaq (Divorce)

Surah At-Talaq has twelve verses and was revealed in Medina. The first seven verses of this Surah concern the subject of divorce and its related rulings and issues, and for this reason, it is named "At-Talaq" (Divorce). The second part of the Surah describes the fate of two groups: first, those who disobeyed God's command and received severe punishment; second, those who, through righteous deeds and following the Prophets, were included in special divine guidance and the blessings of Paradise.

Ayah

فَإِذَا بَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ فَارِقُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ وَأَشْهِدُوا۟ ذَوَىْ عَدْلٍ مِّنكُمْ وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلشَّهَـٰدَةَ لِلَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ يُوعَظُ بِهِۦ مَن كَانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُۥ مَخْرَجًا (۲)
Then, when they have completed their term, either retain them honourably or separate from them honourably, and take the witness of two honest men from among yourselves, and bear witness for the sake of Allah. Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day is advised to [comply with] this. Whoever is wary of Allah, He shall make for him a way out [of the adversities of the world and the Hereafter] (2)

Ayah

وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ ۚ وَمَن يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بَـٰلِغُ أَمْرِهِۦ ۚ قَدْ جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ لِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدْرًا (۳)
and provide for him from whence he does not count upon. And whoever puts his trust in Allah, He will suffice him. Indeed Allah carries through His commands. Certainly, Allah has ordained a measure [and extent] for everything. (3)

Key Points

The meaning of the term "reaching the term" (بلغن اجلهنّ) refers to reaching the end stages of the waiting period ('iddah), not its absolute conclusion. This is because if the waiting period ends, retaining and keeping the woman (imsaak) becomes unlawful (haram).(15)

The word ma'ruf (known/good) has been used thirty-eight times in the Quran, fifteen of which relate to family and marriage, meaning the manner of interaction between husband and wife in life must be appropriate and commendable.

The testimony of two just witnesses to a divorce has benefits. Among them is that the two just witnesses, due to their justice and popularity, naturally try to encourage reconciliation between the parties through admonition instead of divorce.

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «انّى لأعلم آیة لو اخذ بها النّاس لكفتهم» (I know an Ayah that if people were to act upon it, it would suffice them for all their needs). He then recited the Ayah: «و من یتق اللّه یجعل له مخرجاً» (And whoever fears Allah—He will make for him a way out).(16)

We read in narrations: When Ayah 3 was revealed, a group of the Prophet's companions abandoned work and trade, saying: Allah has guaranteed our sustenance, so there is no need for work and effort. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله summoned and reprimanded them, saying: Whoever abandons work and effort, his prayer will not be answered. «انّه من فعل ذلك لا یستجاب له»(17)

In the Quran, both Divine Mercy is mentioned with the phrase, «من حیث لا یحتسب» (from where he does not expect)(18), and Divine Wrath: «فاتاهم اللّه من حیث لم یحتسبوا» (So Allah came upon them from where they had not expected)(19). This means that sometimes Allah's Mercy and Wrath reach a person unexpectedly and in a manner they do not anticipate.

The effect of piety (taqwa) is not only in the Hereafter. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding the phrase, «من حیث لا یحتسب»: This expansion of sustenance is for the pious in this world.(20)

15) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 16) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. 17) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 18) At-Talaq, 3. 19) Al-Hashr, 2. 20) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. The timing of religious rulings must be strictly observed. (Both the start of the 'iddah period: «فطلقوهنّ لعدتهنّ» and its end: «اذا بلغن اجلهن» are mentioned.)

  2. Actions must have a specific plan and time from the beginning. «اجلهنّ»

  3. Both the continuation of life and separation/divorce must be done in a commendable manner. «امسكوهنّ بمعروف او فارقوهنّ بمعروف»

  4. Appropriate behavior, whether in a state of peace and reconciliation or in a state of anger and separation, is a right of the spouse. «امسكوهنّ بمعروف...»

  5. Retaining the wife is prioritized over separation. He first said: «امسكوهنّ» (retain them) and then «فارقوهنّ» (separate from them).

  6. The rules of marriage or divorce must be acceptable to the Sharia and approved by reason. «بمعروف»

  7. Due to physical weakness, social and historical customs, women's rights have been and continue to be violated more often. Therefore, most of the Quranic addresses regarding the observance of rights are directed at men. «امسكوهنّ بمعروف، فارقوهنّ بمعروف»

  8. Separation and divorce must occur with dignity and far from any form of humiliation. «فارقوهنّ بمعروف»

  9. The condition for the validity of divorce is the presence of two just witnesses. «و اشهدوا ذوى عدل منكم»

  10. Taking two just witnesses is a sign of thoroughness and precision for the preservation of the rights of both parties. «و اشهدوا ذوى عدل منكم»

  11. Those in whose presence the divorce is pronounced must be just. «ذوى عدلٍ منكم» (meaning justice must be their capital/essence).

  12. Even just individuals need reminders. «اشهدوا ذوى عدل منكم و اقیموا الشهادة للّه»

  13. Love and hatred must not influence our judgment. «اقیموا الشهادة للّه» (A similar phrase appears in Ayah 135 of Surah An-Nisa, which says: «كونوا قوامین بالقسط شهداء للّه و لو على انفسكم او الوالدین والاقربین»)

  14. The testimony of two just witnesses must be accompanied by the preservation of people's rights and sincerity. «اقیموا الشّهادة للّه» (In the word «اقیموا» (establish) is the establishment of people's rights, and in the word «للّه» (for Allah) is sincerity in action).

  15. The establishment of people's rights has a value equal to the establishment of prayer and religion. (In the Quran, the word «اقیموا» (establish) is used regarding prayer, religion, testimony, and measure: «اقیموا الدین»(21), «اقیموا الصّلاة»(22), «اقیموا الوزن»(23) and «اقیموا الشّهادة»(24))

  16. Admonition (Maw'izah) is not only moral reminders; rather, jurisprudential instructions are also a type of admonition. «امسكوهنّ... فارقوهنّ... اشهدوا... ذلكم یوعظ به»

  17. Accepting advice is a sign of true faith. «یوعظ به من كان یؤمن»

  18. Instead of traversing unknown paths of spiritual journey (sayr wa suluk), acting upon Divine rulings is the best admonition and path for human development. «یوعظ به من كان یؤمن»

  19. Solving problems through sin is going astray. Piety and fear of Allah lead to a person's exit from life's problems and difficulties. «و من یتّق اللّه یجعل له مخرجاً»

  20. The way out of all dead ends (including marital disputes) is piety. «و من یتّق اللّه یجعل له مخرجاً»

  21. In managing crises and tribulations (fitan), two things are the path to salvation: A) Piety. «و من یتّق اللّه یجعل له مخرجاً» B) Adherence to the Quran. «اذا التبست علیكم الفتن كقطع اللیل المظلم فعلیكم بالقرآن»(25)

  22. A husband and wife who are forced to separate through divorce, if they observe piety, will be covered by Allah's special favor. «یرزقه من حیث لایحتسب»

  23. Those who secure their livelihood through sin close the door to receiving unexpected sustenance. «و من یتق اللّه... یرزقه من حیث لایحتسب»

  24. Allah's Will is not confined within human calculations. «یرزقه من حیث لایحتسب»

  25. Observing spiritual matters is effective in material life. (Unseen Divine aid plays an effective role in human life.) «من حیث لایحتسب»

  26. The pious person aligns his desire with the Will and Decree of Allah, and since the Divine Will has no dead end, the pious person will also not reach a dead end. «و من یتق اللّه یجعل له مخرجاً»

  27. Abundant sustenance is not always dependent on abundant work. «و من یتق اللّه... یرزقه» (Just as its quantity is not always important. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in interpreting «یرزقه من حیث لایحتسب», said: Allah blesses what He has given him.(26))

  28. Piety (taqwa) and reliance (tawakkul) are two levers for exiting a dead end. «و من یتق اللّه... و من یتوكل على اللّه...»

  29. For securing today's livelihood, piety is necessary: «و من یتق اللّه... یرزقه». And for the future, reliance (tawakkul) is necessary: «و من یتوكل على اللّه فهو حسبه»

  30. Piety precedes reliance. «و من یتق اللّه... و من یتوكل على اللّه»

  31. Without Allah's favor, no other factor is effective or sufficient. «فهو حسبه»

  32. In the will and desire of all individuals and governments, there is the possibility of failure, ineffectiveness, and dead ends. The only matter in which failure has no place is the Will and Decree of Allah. «انّ اللّه بالغ امره»

  33. The reason for our reliance (tawakkul) is the infinite Power of Allah. «و من یتوكل ... انّ اللّه بالغ امره»

  34. The meaning of our reliance and Allah's Power is not that a person achieves all his desires, because all matters of existence are governed by law and have measure and calculation. «و من یتوكل على اللّه فهو حسبه ان اللّه بالغ امره قد جعل اللّه لكل شى ءٍ قدراً»

21) Ash-Shura, 13.

22) Al-Baqarah, 43.

23) Ar-Rahman, 9.

24) At-Talaq, 2.

25) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 598.

26) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Ayah

وَٱلَّـٰٓـِٔى يَئِسْنَ مِنَ ٱلْمَحِيضِ مِن نِّسَآئِكُمْ إِنِ ٱرْتَبْتُمْ فَعِدَّتُهُنَّ ثَلَـٰثَةُ أَشْهُرٍ وَٱلَّـٰٓـِٔى لَمْ يَحِضْنَ ۚ وَأُو۟لَـٰتُ ٱلْأَحْمَالِ أَجَلُهُنَّ أَن يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَّ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُۥ مِنْ أَمْرِهِۦ يُسْرًا (٤)
As for those of your wives who have ceased having menses—[or] if you have any doubts [concerning its cause, whether it’s is age or something else]—their term [of waiting] and of those who have not yet had menses, shall be three months. As for those who are pregnant, their term shall be until they deliver. And whoever is wary of Allah, He shall grant him ease in his affairs. (4)

Key Points

Following the explanation of the rulings of divorce in the preceding verses, this verse states: Women who, for any reason—whether old age or illness—do not have menstruation, must observe a waiting period (‘iddah) of three full months. Pregnant women must wait until the time of delivery, after which they may marry and take another husband. Several expressions are used in the Quran for pious individuals: «یجعل لكم نوراً» (27), «یجعل لكم فرقاناً» (28), «یجعل له مخرجاً» (29), «یجعل له من امره یُسراً» (30). This perhaps points to a natural progression, because for the pious person, light (nūr) first appears, and in the shadow of that light, he gains the recognition of truth from falsehood (furqān). Once he recognizes truth and falsehood, he finds a way out of difficulties, and in the shadow of that same light, he easily escapes many troubles.

27) Hadid, 28. 28) Anfal, 29. 29) Talaq, 2. 30) Talaq, 4.

Messages

1- Allah’s rulings differ based on the varying conditions of people. (A specific ruling has been established for each of the pregnant and menopausal women.) «یئسن...، لم یحضن و اولات الاحمال»

2- The innate religion has innate, natural, and universal standards. The timing and measurements of divine rulings revolve around natural matters, such as: the rising and setting of the sun, the sighting of the moon, and the measure of a hand span (wajib) and cubit (dhirā‘). «ثلاثة اشهر»

3- The child has sanctity and rights, and a pregnant woman cannot marry another man. «اولات الاحمال اجلهنّ ان یضعن حملهنّ»

4- In family matters and observing the rights of the spouse, especially during divorce and separation, great piety (taqwa) is needed. (The discussion of piety has been raised several times from the beginning of this Surah until now.) «و من یتّق اللّه»

5- Part of the knots that appear in life are due to lack of piety. «و من یتق اللّه یجعل له من امره یسراً» (Of course, some difficulties faced by pious individuals are due to trial and testing.)

6- For pious individuals, enduring hardships is easy, because piety raises human capacity. «یجعل له من امره یسراً» («من امره» means: "from the aspect of his affairs.")

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ أَنزَلَهُۥٓ إِلَيْكُمْ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ يُكَفِّرْ عَنْهُ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِۦ وَيُعْظِمْ لَهُۥٓ أَجْرًا (٥)
That is Allah’s ordinance, which He has sent down to you, and whoever is wary of Allah, He shall absolve him of his misdeeds and give him a great reward. (5)

Messages

1- In family matters, we should not view divine commands lightly. «ذلك امر اللّه انزله الیكم»

2- Piety (Taqwa) is the cause of salvation from divine wrath. «من یتق اللّه یكفّر عنه سیئاته»

3- Sinners can compensate for the past through piety (Taqwa). «یكفّر عنه سیئاته»

4- Do not assume that sinners will have a bad end; perhaps their sins will be wiped away. «یكفّرعنه سیئاته»

5- The prerequisite for receiving reward is purification from sin. «یكفّر عنه سیئاته و یعظم له اجرا»

6- Piety (Taqwa) both compensates for the past, «یكفر عنه سیئاته», and secures the future. «یعظم له اجراً»

Ayah

أَسْكِنُوهُنَّ مِنْ حَيْثُ سَكَنتُم مِّن وُجْدِكُمْ وَلَا تُضَآرُّوهُنَّ لِتُضَيِّقُوا۟ عَلَيْهِنَّ ۚ وَإِن كُنَّ أُو۟لَـٰتِ حَمْلٍ فَأَنفِقُوا۟ عَلَيْهِنَّ حَتَّىٰ يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَّ ۚ فَإِنْ أَرْضَعْنَ لَكُمْ فَـَٔاتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ ۖ وَأْتَمِرُوا۟ بَيْنَكُم بِمَعْرُوفٍ ۖ وَإِن تَعَاسَرْتُمْ فَسَتُرْضِعُ لَهُۥٓ أُخْرَىٰ (٦)
House them where you live, in accordance with your means, and do not harass them to put them in straits, and should they be pregnant, maintain them until they deliver. Then, if they suckle [the baby] for you, give them their wages and consult together honourably. But if you make things difficult for each other, then another woman will suckle [the baby] for him. (6)

Key Points

«أتمروا» (Atamarū) is derived from ‘ītimār’, meaning the acceptance of an instruction or consultation, which essentially means agreement and understanding regarding the execution of a matter. Perhaps the reason why the dwelling place of the man and woman is the same during the waiting period (‘iddah’) is to pave the way for reconciliation and create the grounds for reunion. Furthermore, expelling the woman from the house or providing separate accommodation is a form of insult and humiliation against the woman. Also, in gradual separation, reason, emotion, thought, and consultation play a greater role than in sudden and immediate separation, making reconciliation possible. This Ayah refers to the issue of preserving the rights and sanctity of the woman, her pregnancy, the child's nourishment, the nursing fee, and hiring a wet nurse in case of divorce. Five commands are mentioned in this Ayah, all of which are emotional and rational directives, paving the way for a return to life: «اسكنوهنّ, لاتضاروهنّ, فانفقوا علیهنّ, فاتوهنّ اجورهنّ, ائتمروا بینكم بالمعروف»

Messages

1- Providing accommodation for the woman, even after divorce, remains the responsibility of the man for a period. «اسكنوهنّ»

2- The accommodation of the divorced woman must be in the same place where the man resides. (Separation and divorce should not lead to separation of residence). «من حیث سكنتم»

3- The man does not have the right to choose an unsuitable place for the woman's residence—for the sake of saving money, revenge, or humiliation—and force the divorced wife to reside there. «من حیث سكنتم»

4- In choosing accommodation, attention must be paid to the woman's sakīnah (tranquility) and peace, not just that she has shelter. «اسكنوهنّ من حیث سكنتم» (The use of the term maskan [dwelling], which derives from sakīnah [tranquility], instead of bayt [house], indicates this meaning.)

5- The criterion for choosing accommodation, in terms of quantity and quality, is the extent of the man's ability, not the level of the woman's expectation and demand. «من وجدكم»

6- The man does not have the right to inflict any harm upon the wife he has divorced. «لاتضاروهنّ»

7- The man does not have the right to impose any kind of hardship upon the wife he has divorced. «لتضیّقوهنّ»

8- Providing expenses for pregnant women, even after divorce, is obligatory. «فانفقوا علیهنّ حتّى یضعن حملهنّ»

9- A divorced woman may request wages from her husband for breastfeeding their child. «فاتوهنّ اجورهنّ»

10- Mother's milk, even if she receives wages from the husband, is better than a wet nurse's milk. «فان ارضعن لكم فاتوهنّ اجورهنّ» Furthermore, divorced women are free to choose whether to breastfeed. «فان ارضعن لكم»

11- Consultation between the man and woman is so valuable that Islam has commanded it even under conditions of separation. «وائتمروا بینكم»

12- In all family matters, the criterion for acceptance is custom (‘urf’) and reason. «فامسكوهنّ بمعروف, فارقوهن بمعروف..., وائتمروا بینكم بالمعروف»

13- Consultation is necessary, even if it does not always yield results. «و ان تعاسرتم»

14- If the man and woman do not reach an agreement, the child's rights must not be neglected. «فسترضع له اُخرى »

15- Mother's milk has priority for the child, and deviation from it occurs only if one is in hardship. «و ان تعاسرتم فسترضع له اُخرى »

16- The woman does not have the right to raise the nursing wage so high that the man is put in hardship; if she does so, the man is free to hire a wet nurse. «و ان تعاسرتم فسترضع له اُخرى »

17- In family decisions, the rights of the infant and child must be preserved: a) As long as the child is in the womb, the father must provide for its expenses. b) After birth, the mother should breastfeed the child. c) The father must pay the nursing fee to the mother.

Ayah

لِيُنفِقْ ذُو سَعَةٍ مِّن سَعَتِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن قُدِرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقُهُۥ فَلْيُنفِقْ مِمَّآ ءَاتَىٰهُ ٱللَّهُ ۚ لَا يُكَلِّفُ ٱللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا مَآ ءَاتَىٰهَا ۚ سَيَجْعَلُ ٱللَّهُ بَعْدَ عُسْرٍ يُسْرًا (۷)
Let the affluent man spend out of his affluence, and let he whose provision has been tightened spend out of what Allah has given him. Allah does not task any soul except [according to] what He has given it. Allah will bring about ease after hardship. (7)

Key Points

The command in this Ayah, that every person should spend on his wife according to his ability, includes the days of the waiting period ('iddah), the days of breastfeeding the divorced wife, and also every other time.

Messages

1- The criterion for the amount of living expenses is the financial ability of the man, not the stinginess of the man or the demands or wishes of the woman. «ذوسعةٍ من سعته»

2- Do not make today difficult out of fear of the future. (Whoever has means should spend.) «ذوسعةٍ من سعته»

3- Even in conditions of divorce, do not lose chivalry. «ذوسعة من سعته»

4- The condition for maintenance (nafaqah) is not loving the wife; the divorced woman is also entitled to maintenance for a period. «ذوسعة من سعته»

5- The spender should consider his wealth and possessions to be from Allah. «ممّا اتاه اللّه»

6- Everyone should spend according to the extent of his ability to spend. If one cannot draw the water of the sea, one must at least taste it according to his thirst. «من قدر... فلینفق ممّا اتاه اللّه»

7- The poor, when comparing their situation with the rich, should not forget the blessings that Allah has given them. «قدر علیه... اتاه اللّه»

8- Obligation is according to ability. «لا یكلف اللّه نفساً الاّ ما اتاها» Islam is a realistic religion and does not impose obligations beyond one's capacity.

9- Do not rush in achieving ease (or relief). «سیجعل اللّه»

10- In the bitter conditions of divorce and separation, or poverty and hardship, do not lose hope in the future. «سیجعل اللّه بعد عسر یُسراً»

Ayah

وَكَأَيِّن مِّن قَرْيَةٍ عَتَتْ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهَا وَرُسُلِهِۦ فَحَاسَبْنَـٰهَا حِسَابًا شَدِيدًا وَعَذَّبْنَـٰهَا عَذَابًا نُّكْرًا (۸)
How many a town defied the command of its Lord and His apostles, then We called it to a severe account and punished it with a dire punishment. (8)

Ayah

فَذَاقَتْ وَبَالَ أَمْرِهَا وَكَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ أَمْرِهَا خُسْرًا (۹)
So it tasted the evil consequences of its conduct, and the outcome of its conduct was ruin. (9)

Key Points

Qaryah (village/town) means the place where people gather for residence, whether it is a city or a village. ‘Atat (it rebelled) is derived from ‘Utuww, meaning disobedience (or rebellion/defiance). Wabāl (consequence/severity) means intensity and heaviness, and the intention here is severe punishment. In the previous verses, instructions regarding the rights of the wife and child, even during periods of separation, were discussed. This verse states: Take the implementation of these instructions seriously, for deviation from the command of Allah and the Messenger entails a severe penalty, and many examples of this penalty can be observed throughout history.

Messages

1- History and past generations are a mirror for future generations. «و كایّن من قریة...»

2- The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is to severely punish those who disobey the command of Allah and the Messenger. «و كایّن من قریة عتت...»

3- Deviation from the command of the Prophets is deviation from the command of Allah. «عتت عن امر ربّها و رسله»

4- The punishment of this world, no matter how severe, is merely a taste. The important punishment is in the Hereafter. «عذبناها... فذاقت وبال امرها»

5- The ultimate outcome of disobeying the command of Allah and the Messenger is loss. «عاقبة امرها خسراً»

6- Let us not dwell on fleeting successes; let us consider the end result. «عاقبة امرها خُسراً»

Ayah

أَعَدَّ ٱللَّهُ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا ۖ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ يَـٰٓأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ۚ قَدْ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ إِلَيْكُمْ ذِكْرًا (۱٠)
Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment. So be wary of Allah, O you who possess intellect and have faith! Allah has already sent down to you a reminder, (10)

Messages

1- Allah's severe reckoning (judgment) is followed by severe punishment. «حساباً شدیداً... عذاباً شدیداً»

2- What protects man from severe reckoning and punishment is piety (Taqwa). «اعدّ اللّه لهم عذاباً شدیداً فاتقوا اللّه»

3- Intellect (Aql) and faith are the cause of piety (Taqwa). «فاتقوا اللّه یا اولى الالباب الّذین آمنوا»

4- Intellect and faith are inseparable. «اولى الالباب الّذین آمنوا»

5- To encourage people to fulfill their divine duties, mention their virtues (perfections). «فاتقوا اللّه یا اولى الالباب الّذین آمنوا»

6- Only the believing people of understanding welcome the Messenger of Allah and benefit from His reminders. «یا اولى الالباب الّذین آمنوا قد انزل اللّه الیكم ذكرا»

7- The purpose of the mission of the prophets and the heavenly books is to remind and awaken people. «قد انزل اللّه الیكم ذكراً»

8- Intellect (Aql) from within and revelation (Wahy) from without are the means of human salvation. «اولى الالباب... انزل اللّه الیكم ذكراً»

Ayah

رَّسُولًا يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ مُبَيِّنَـٰتٍ لِّيُخْرِجَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ مِنَ ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ إِلَى ٱلنُّورِ ۚ وَمَن يُؤْمِنۢ بِٱللَّهِ وَيَعْمَلْ صَـٰلِحًا يُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۖ قَدْ أَحْسَنَ ٱللَّهُ لَهُۥ رِزْقًا (۱۱)
an apostle reciting to you the manifest signs of Allah that He may bring out those who have faith and do righteous deeds from darkness into light. And whoever has faith in Allah and does righteous deeds, He shall admit him into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them forever. Allah has certainly granted him an excellent provision. (11)

Key Points

Guidance depends on several elements: A) The guide, "رسولاً" (a Messenger). B) Those being guided, "الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات" (those who believe and do righteous deeds). C) The means of guidance, "آیات بیّنات" (clear signs/verses). D) The goal of guidance, "یخرجهم من الظلمات الى النور" (He brings them out of the darknesses into the light). E) The reward for accepting guidance, "یدخله جنّات..." (He will admit him to Gardens...).

The word "ذکر" (Dhikr/Remembrance) is used both for the Quran, "نحن نزّلنا الذكر" (We have sent down the Dhikr) (31), and for the Prophet. "ذكراً رسولاً یتلوا..." (a Dhikr, a Messenger who recites...). Aisha, the wife of the noble Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, says in describing the Prophet: "كان خلقه قرآن" (His character was the Quran) (32). Meaning, the character and disposition of the Prophet were entirely the Quran.

31) Hijr, 9. 32) Sharh Nahj al-Balaghah by Ibn Abi al-Hadid, Vol. 6, p. 340.

Messages

1- A person can reach a point where his entire being is the remembrance (Dhikr) of Allah. "ذكراً رسولاً" (a Dhikr, a Messenger).

2- The school of thought (the doctrine) alone is not sufficient; the presence of a leader is necessary. "رسولاً یتلوا" (a Messenger who recites).

3- For the salvation and emergence of people from darknesses into light, the best way is the recitation and conveyance of Divine verses. "یتلوا علیكم آیات اللّه... لیخرج الّذین آمنوا..." (He recites to you the verses of Allah... that He may bring forth those who believe...).

4- The call (Da'wah) must be clear and transparent. "آیات اللّه مبیّنات" (clear verses of Allah). (The word "Mubin" [clear/manifest] has been used more than a hundred times in the Quran, such as: "البلاغ المبین" (the clear conveyance) (33), "قرآن مبین" (a clear Quran) (34), "سلطاناً مبیناً" (a clear authority) (35)).

5- The paths of falsehood, polytheism, and disbelief are numerous, but the path of truth is only one. "الظلمات... النّور" (the darknesses... the Light). ("ظلمات" [darknesses] is plural, and "نور" [light] is singular).

6- The goal of the Prophets is the salvation of humanity. "رسولاً یتلوا... لیخرج..." (a Messenger who recites... that He may bring forth...).

7- Bringing people out of darknesses is fundamentally the work of Allah, "اللّه ولىّ الّذین آمنوا یخرجهم من الظلمات الى النّور" (Allah is the Protector of those who believe; He brings them out from the darknesses into the light) (36), but this work is accomplished through the Prophets. "رسولاً... لیخرج" (a Messenger... that He may bring forth).

8- Although the goal of the Prophets is to bring all people out of darknesses into the light, "لتخرج النّاس من الظلمات الى النّور" (that you may bring forth mankind from the darknesses into the light) (37), this goal is realized only concerning the believers. "لیخرج الّذین آمنوا... من الظلمات الى النّور" (that He may bring forth those who believe... from the darknesses into the light).

9- Even after faith and righteous deeds, a person dwells in darknesses from which he must emerge. "لیخرج الّذین آمنوا... من الظلمات الى النّور" (that He may bring forth those who believe... from the darknesses into the light).

10- Faith and action are constantly coupled together. "یؤمن باللّه و یعمل صالحاً" (who believes in Allah and does righteous deeds).

11- Encouragement and warning must be balanced. "عذاباً نكراً" (a dreadful punishment)... "احسن اللّه له رزقاً" (Allah will grant him an excellent provision).

33) Ma'idah, 92.

34) Hijr, 1.

35) Nisa', 91.

36) Baqarah, 257.

37) Ibrahim, 1.

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَمَـٰوَٰتٍ وَمِنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ مِثْلَهُنَّ يَتَنَزَّلُ ٱلْأَمْرُ بَيْنَهُنَّ لِتَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَدْ أَحَاطَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عِلْمًۢا (۱۲)
It is Allah who has created seven heavens, and of the earth [a number] similar to them. The command gradually descends through them, that you may know that Allah has power over all things, and that Allah comprehends all things in knowledge. (12)

Key Points

The meaning of the seven earths (or seven lands) is one of several possibilities: (38) A) Seven celestial spheres (planets) that are structurally similar to the Earth. B) The seven layers (strata) of the Earth, which are stacked upon one another like the layers of an onion. C) The seven climes (regions) of the Earth, into which ancient geographers divided the Earth into seven parts or continents.

In this Ayah, the creation of the heavens and the earth and the management of their affairs are presented as a means for human beings to become aware of Divine knowledge and power, and to achieve monotheistic understanding and growth. "لتعلموا..." (that you may know...)

In Ayah 7 of Surah Hud, the purpose of the creation of the heavens and the earth is stated to be the testing of humanity. "و هو الذى خلق السموات و الارض فى ستّة ایّام... لیبلوكم ایّكم احسن عملاً" (And He it is Who created the heavens and the earth in six days... that He might test you, which of you is best in deed.)

And in Ayah 119 of the same Surah, Divine Mercy is mentioned as the basis for the creation of humanity. "الاّ من رحم ربّك و لذلك خلقهم" (Except those on whom your Lord has bestowed His mercy; and for that did He create them.)

And Ayah 56 of Surah Adh-Dhariyat considers the purpose of human creation to be the worship of God (Allah). "و ما خلقت الجن و الانس الاّ لیعبدون" (And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.)

From the totality of these verses, it can be concluded that the purpose of the creation of existence and humanity is knowledge, action, worship, and the reception of Divine Mercy.

38) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Be assured that Allah fulfills His promises, for He possesses such power that He created existence itself. "یدخله جنات، احسن اللّه له رزقا، اللّه الذى خلق"

2- Existence possesses a type of balance. "سبع سموات و من الارض مثلهنّ"

3- Both the creation of existence and the management of its affairs belong to Allah. "خلق... یتنزل الامر"

4- The Divine Command (امر) is sometimes legislative (تشریعى), "عتت عن امر ربّها" (it rebelled against the command of its Lord) (39), and sometimes creative/existential (تكوینى). "یتنزل الامر بینهنّ"

5- Existence is a classroom. "خلق... لتعلموا"

6- Human perfection and growth are the axis of all creation. "لتعلموا"

7- Islam pays attention to human knowledge regarding the management of the system of creation. "خلق سبع سموات... یتنزل الامر... لتعلموا..."

8- The One Who is the Creator and Originator can have complete comprehensive knowledge (encompassment) over His creation. "خلق... احاط بكلّ شى ء علماً"

9- Allah's knowledge of everything is complete, precise, and flawless. "احاط بكلّ شى ء علماً"

39) At-Talaq, 8.
